estimate_rationale: Sum of per-feature estimated_lines (031..045). Framework-side of handover NIP-0001 (F-001..F-015): one NIP + planning activation, one module index, four protocol docs, three JSON schemas (each with positive + negative fixtures), capability routing across NIZAM.json/tools, one deterministic preflight CLI, a validator + CI fixtures extension, two dogfood-evidence features, and a human-gated release. Docs and schemas dominate; the CLI (041) and validator extension (042) carry the largest code deltas. Consumer adoption (F-016..F-020) is excluded from this figure as successor-phase scope.

provenance.out_of_scope: Handover F-016..F-020 (consumer adoption) deferred to the successor programme phase.

features.name: NIP-0001 Acceptance + Phase-005 Plan of Record (PR-F1)
features.description: Handover F-001 (Accept NIP-0001). Land the accepted NIP-0001 and the phase-005 plan of record: docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md with body status Accepted and an acceptance record (operator, 2026-07-17, verbatim authorization); the phase-005 planning artifacts (.agent/product_spec_005.md, .agent/feature_list_005.json); the manifest phase-005 activation entry; and the ROADMAP update marking phase 005 the active plan of record. Frontmatter status is 'active' (frontmatter schema enum is draft/active/deprecated); the decision-lifecycle state 'Accepted' is carried in the body, matching the docs/architecture/ADR house pattern. No ecosystem module/schema/tooling work in this feature.
acceptance_tests: NIP exists with status accepted after operator approval
NIP paths and frontmatter validate
NIP file exists: test -f docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md

NIP frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"

NIP body records the Accepted decision, the operator, the 2026-07-17 date, and the verbatim authorization: python3 -c "t=open('docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('Accepted' in t and '2026-07-17' in t and 'approved. expedite.' in t) else 1)"

manifest.json indexes phase 005 with its feature list path and current_phase points at it: python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load(open('docs/planning/manifest.json')); ids=[p['id'] for p in m['phases']]; ok=any(i.startswith('005') for i in ids) and m['current_phase'].startswith('005'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)"

manifest.json still validates against schema/manifest.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('docs/planning/manifest.json')), json.load(open('schema/manifest.schema.json')))"

feature_list_005.json validates against schema/feature_list.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/feature_list_005.json')), json.load(open('schema/feature_list.schema.json')))"

[AMENDED 2026-07-17 per contract 031 rev1 / qa/031-contract-review.json: the original command `grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` was a reproduced false-pass at HEAD (six pre-existing 'phase 005 candidate scope' references); superseded by contract-031 verification entries 7-8, which require the literal 'Plan of Record (2026-07-17)' banner AND the Track-2-heading-anchored SUPERSEDED marker, with an anti-vacuity companion proving the check fails at HEAD] ROADMAP.md marks phase 005 as the active plan of record (not merely candidate scope): grep -Eqi '005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md
Existing compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Immutable Baseline Protocol (PR-F2)

Author ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md defining a point-in-time baseline over framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence references, with explicit fields and evidence rules. A baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions (every fact is anchored to a stated SHA/timestamp).

Baseline cannot mix evidence from unspecified revisions

The protocol doc states baseline fields and the revision/timestamp anchoring rule: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if ('baseline' in t and ('revision' in t or 'sha' in t) and ('timestamp' in t or 'point-in-time' in t)) else 1)"

features.name: Baseline Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2)
features.description: Handover F-007 (Add baseline schema). Add schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json describing the immutable baseline artifact (framework/repository/dependency/CI/planning/evidence references with revision anchors), index it in NIZAM.json, and add positive + negative fixtures under tools/fixtures/. The positive fixture validates; the negative fixture (e.g. baseline mixing states without timestamps) fails.
acceptance_tests: Positive fixture validates
Negative fixture fails
The schema is itself a valid JSON Schema: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json')))"

A positive baseline fixture validates AND a negative baseline fixture is rejected: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema,sys; s=json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
import glob
pos=[f for f in glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*') if 'valid' in f or 'pos' in f]
neg=[f for f in glob.glob('tools/fixtures/*baseline*') if 'invalid' in f or 'neg' in f]
assert pos and neg, 'need positive and negative baseline fixtures'
jsonschema.validate(json.load(open(pos[0])), s)
try:
  jsonschema.validate(json.load(open(neg[0])), s); sys.exit(1)
except jsonschema.ValidationError:
  sys.exit(0)"
NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green: grep -q 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

features.name: Minimum Preflight CLI (PR-F3)

Add tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, the minimum deterministic preflight tool: it emits schema-valid preflight and baseline artifacts, supports a framework-self fixture mode (so the framework can dogfood against itself), and documents its failure exit codes. Composes evidence externalisation per the framework's tool-driven-state convention.

CLI supports framework-self fixture mode
Failure exit codes are documented
The CLI exists and is syntactically valid Python: test -f tools/ecosystem_preflight.py && python3 -m py_compile tools/ecosystem_preflight.py

Run in framework-self fixture mode emits a preflight artifact that validates against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and a baseline artifact that validates against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (evidence captured by path under .agent/evidence/)

Failure exit codes are documented in the CLI (help text or module docstring): python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help 2>&1 | grep -Eqi 'exit|code|fail' || python3 -c "import ast,sys; t=ast.get_docstring(ast.parse(open('tools/ecosystem_preflight.py').read())); sys.exit(0 if t and ('exit' in t.lower()) else 1)"
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'

The hermetic e2e bootstrap test still passes: bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1; test $? -eq 0

features.name: Release Framework Capability — HUMAN GATE (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE)
features.description: Handover F-015 (Release framework capability). Prepare the framework release of the ecosystem cycle: classify the version correctly (expected MINOR per methodology/06_release_train.md — additive module/schemas/tooling, no breaking runtime change), update CHANGELOG.md and the user guide, obtain human sign-off, and publish an immutable tag. This is a HUMAN GATE (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE): the pipeline prepares and records the release but does NOT self-cut the tag or self-approve. Release exit criteria (validation green, dogfood complete, no unresolved P0/P1 in the new capability) must hold first.
acceptance_tests: Version classified correctly
Changelog and guide updated
Human sign-off complete
Immutable tag published

CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] (or the release section) names the ecosystem-cycle deliverables and the classified version: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('CHANGELOG.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); sys.exit(0 if 'ecosystem' in t else 1)"
The release tag is NOT auto-cut by the pipeline (human gate): the release is recorded but the tag creation and sign-off are performed by a human with release authority
Release exit criteria hold: compliance validator green and no unresolved P0/P1 in the new capability recorded as Open in docs/planning/DEBT.md
Compliance validator stays green: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed'

features.name: PR-stack review response (round 1: PR #20)
features.description: Review-response overhead feature (not a handover deliverable): resolve CodeRabbit's six actionable comments on PR #20 (CHANGES_REQUESTED) as a single new commit at the stack tip, per the stacked-PR convention (earlier PR threads resolved by reference to the tip commit). The six findings were AH-2 triaged against the live tree at HEAD ada3b9c into three dispositions. FIX (two live truth-sync defects, both confirmed still present): (1) `.agent/product_spec_005.md` frontmatter `status: draft` contradicts its own body ('Status: ACTIVE — plan of record') and the manifest (phase 005 active since 2026-07-17) — flip status to 'active' (frontmatter enum draft/active/deprecated) with a house-pattern version bump + change_log entry; (2) `docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` Sequencing Recommendation ('Track 2 is the recommended phase 005') contradicts the same file's own supersession note (Track 2 was SUPERSEDED as the phase-005 selection by NIP-0001) — rewrite the sentence to frame Track 2 as candidate scope for a subsequent phase, with a ROADMAP version bump + change_log entry. REGISTER AS DEBT (no code change now; both rows MUST be severity Low or Medium so 045's release-readiness verification entry 18 (zero Open P0/P1) stays satisfied on re-run): NDEBT-019 (Low) — the `bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep ...` authoring pattern lacks pipefail/explicit exit-code capture so the validator's own exit status is not asserted (false-PASS only theoretical: a failing sweep emits no '0 failed' SUMMARY line); hardening deferred to the methodology/02 verification-authoring standard, historical evidence captures are IMMUTABLE; NDEBT-020 (Medium) — during 045's contract review round 1 the evaluator misfired `rm -f` at the real CHANGELOG.md during a probe (self-caught and restored same turn via git checkout, zero lasting effect, disclosed in `.agent/qa/045-contract-review.json`); registers the incident plus the strengthened probe-isolation rule (verify cwd before any destructive command; destructive commands must never carry real-repo paths). EXPLAIN-AND-RESOLVE (no repo change; deliverable is documented resolution rationale the orchestrator posts as PR reply comments — source text in `.agent/planning_notes_046.md`): comment on `.agent/evidence/031-verify-04.txt` (looseness already catalogued in NDEBT-014, immutable point-in-time capture of committed-and-correct manifest state); comment on `.agent/feature_list_005.json` acceptance checks 'not load-bearing' (REFUTED: `tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_exceptions.json` IS the PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-without-operator_approval negative fixture, invalid_approval_incomplete covers the incomplete case, C12 asserts every invalid_ fixture is rejected each sweep; capability-path concern enforced by C9+C10 on NIZAM.json); comment on `ecosystem/README.md` 'stub in PR-F1' (shipped complete-for-031 and completed by 032-040 within this stack — at the stack tip it is v0.2.0/active/complete; PR-F1 history cannot be rewritten without invalidating SHA-pinned dogfood baselines). SCOPE: exactly three files — `.agent/product_spec_005.md` (status flip + its own change_log only; the 046 rationale lives in THIS feature entry, NOT in the spec body, so the spec stays stable while under review in PR #20), `docs/planning/ROADMAP.md` (one-sentence staleness fix + version/change_log), `docs/planning/DEBT.md` (two new rows + version bump + register-section prose per house format). NOTHING else: no evidence-file edits (immutable), no touch to the SHA-pinned `.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md`, no methodology edits (deferred via NDEBT-019), no NIZAM/README/CHANGELOG edits. Both compliance sweeps must stay green (12/12 default, 10/10 payload). Ordering note: depends on the committed 045 preparation (ada3b9c) but must LAND BEFORE the operator executes H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE — review-response fixes precede the merge/tag; 045's completion (human sign-off + tag) comes after.
acceptance_tests: Spec frontmatter status flipped from draft to active with a new change_log entry, still schema-valid
ROADMAP Sequencing Recommendation no longer recommends Track 2 as the phase-005 selection; ROADMAP version bumped with a change_log entry
DEBT.md registers exactly NDEBT-019 and NDEBT-020, both severity Low or Medium, with a version bump
The tip commit touches ONLY the three allowed files; both compliance sweeps stay green

.agent/product_spec_005.md frontmatter status is 'active' and the change_log grew: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('.agent/product_spec_005.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]); sys.exit(0 if d['status']=='active' and len(d.get('change_log',[]))>=2 else 1)"

.agent/product_spec_005.md frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('.agent/product_spec_005.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))"
The stale ROADMAP sentence is gone at HEAD: ! grep -Eq 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005' docs/planning/ROADMAP.md

Anti-vacuity companion (HEAD-anchored, proves the removal check is load-bearing): the stale sentence DID exist on the parent commit: git show HEAD^:docs/planning/ROADMAP.md | grep -Eq 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005'

features.name: PR-stack review response (round 2: PRs #22-#24)
features.description: Review-response overhead feature (not a handover deliverable): resolve CodeRabbit's round-2 comments across PRs #22/#23/#24 (30 comments total; PR #21 was skipped by CodeRabbit — 291 files > 150 limit — no inline comments, surfaced to the operator, no feature action). AH-2 triage completed in .agent/planning_notes_047.md (source: the completed 30-comment triage); do not re-triage. Both FIX items AH-2-re-verified still present at HEAD 6db8363. Disposition breakdown: 2 FIX, 6 DEBT (registered as 2 bundled rows), 20 EXPLAIN + 2 DUPLICATE (22 ready-to-post PR replies in the planning note). FIX 1 (docs/planning/DEBT.md): the NDEBT-016 row's regex code-span `(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)` carries three raw pipes, so GFM parses the row as 8 cells (MD056) — escape the three pipes as `\|` so the row renders back to its correct 5 cells with identical content; the severity cell MUST stay 'Low' in position 3 so 045's committed release-readiness scan (045-verify-18, counts open High/Critical rows via the severity cell) and 046's NDEBT-scan style checks stay green. FIX 2 (tools/validate.sh, DOC-ONLY, ZERO behavior change): reword two inaccurate spots — (a) the help text 'the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read' is false since C12 reads the three ecosystem fixture families in the DEFAULT sweep (validate.sh:1593), and (b) the line-1336 comment '(each fixture is already individually inspectable that way)' contradicts registered NDEBT-015 (all three ecosystem families misroute under --target). Line 319's phrasing ('independently inspectable via a direct python3+jsonschema invocation') is ALREADY correct — the refuted part of the finding — and MUST NOT be touched. Fix now, pre-tag, because validate.sh ships in the v0.7.0 consumer payload. DEBT (no code change now; both rows Low/Medium ONLY so 045-verify-18 stays satisfied): NDEBT-021 (Low) — bundled ecosystem_preflight.py minimum-CLI hardening backlog (porcelain-quoting robustness — NOTE it fails CLOSED, and a naive `-z --untracked-files=all` fix would break the directory-prefix exception semantics the committed 043/044 approved exception sets rely on; os.path.exists()/unknown-HEAD edge; explicit `--repo-root '.'` indistinguishable from default; stale output-dir artifacts on reuse; plus the executed untracked-rejection probe note), deferred post-v0.7.0 alongside NDEBT-017; NDEBT-022 (Medium) — ecosystem/07 progress-comparison Sec 3's four-class transition taxonomy is non-exhaustive (no class for a persisting finding with freshly re-confirmed evidence, none for a pre-window-resolved finding) and the delta score fields count recorded-not-open findings; the audit-044 delta bridged both transparently with explicit notes (nothing hidden, +2 movement correct and cited), so the immutable committed delta artifact needs NO change — the fix is amending the LIVE ecosystem/07 protocol doc in a future phase. Explain-and-resolve (no repo change; deliverable is documented rationale the orchestrator posts as PR replies, source in .agent/planning_notes_047.md §replies): all 20 EXPLAIN comments (immutable point-in-time evidence captures, HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity house pattern, non-goal-guard-not-allowlist semantics, single-directory-at-capture determinism, burden-of-staleness fail-safe, orchestrator-owned run_state resolved-at-tip, README/CHANGELOG release-package convention) plus the 2 DUPLICATE cross-references (044-verify-19 dup of 043-verify-17; 045-verify-16 dup of 042-verify-14). CHANGELOG [0.7.0] date-vs-tag-date is NOT touched here — carried by the orchestrator as a release-day note to the operator (release.yml extraction is date-agnostic, prefix-match). SCOPE: exactly two files — docs/planning/DEBT.md and tools/validate.sh — NOTHING else: no evidence-file edits (immutable), no touch to the SHA-pinned .agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md or the release-notes hash, no run_state edit, no tags, no NIZAM/README/CHANGELOG/ecosystem_preflight.py/ecosystem-doc edits. Ordering: lands as a single new commit at the stack tip (depends on the committed 046 close-out, HEAD 6db8363) and must LAND BEFORE the operator executes H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE — the validate.sh doc fix ships inside the v0.7.0 payload. Both compliance sweeps must stay green (12/12 default, 10/10 payload) and the e2e bootstrap test must stay unaffected.
acceptance_tests: DEBT.md NDEBT-016 row parses to exactly 5 cells with severity 'Low' in position 3 (the raw regex pipes are escaped so GFM no longer mis-splits the row)
tools/validate.sh help text and the C12 comment no longer misstate fixture-read modes; line 319's already-correct phrasing is untouched; zero behavior change
DEBT.md registers exactly NDEBT-021 (Low) and NDEBT-022 (Medium) with a frontmatter version bump
Both sweeps stay green (12/12 default, 10/10 payload), the e2e bootstrap is unaffected, and the tip commit touches only the two allowed files

DEBT.md NDEBT-016 row parses to exactly 5 markdown cells with 'NDEBT-016' in cell 1 and 'Low' in cell 3 (literal escaped pipes neutralised before splitting on the real cell delimiters): python3 -c "import sys; ls=[l for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8') if l.lstrip().startswith('| NDEBT-016 ')]; assert ls, 'row missing'; s=ls[0].strip().replace(chr(92)+'|',chr(0)); cells=s.split('|')[1:-1]; sys.exit(0 if len(cells)==5 and cells[0].strip()=='NDEBT-016' and cells[2].strip()=='Low' else 1)"

The escaped regex code-span is present and the raw-pipe form is gone at HEAD: grep -Fq '(?:^\|_)(?:neg\|invalid)(?:_\|\.)' docs/planning/DEBT.md && ! grep -Fq '(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)' docs/planning/DEBT.md

Anti-vacuity companion (HEAD^-anchored, proves the escape check is load-bearing): the raw-pipe form existed on the parent commit: git show HEAD^:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -Fq '(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)'

validate.sh help text no longer claims --target is the only fixture-read mode (whitespace-collapsed to survive line-wrap, per NDEBT-014 class 3): python3 -c "import sys; t=' '.join(open('tools/validate.sh',encoding='utf-8').read().split()); bad='--target is the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read'; ok='Files in tools/fixtures/ are read only under --target and by the default sweep'; sys.exit(0 if bad not in t and ok in t else 1)"
